title: Epsilon
summary: Epsilon is a medium-difficulty Linux machine that exposes a Git repository on the web server. During enumeration, leaked AWS Lambda credentials are discovered in Git commits, allowing the secret key to sign JWTs. With this key, admin access is obtained, SSTI is exploited for a reverse shell, and finally root is gained by abusing a cronjob running tar with the -h option via symbolic links.

title: Djinn3
summary: Djinn3 is a VulnHub Linux machine with multiple web services. Users enumerate and exploit misconfigured scripts to gain initial access, followed by privilege escalation via SUID binaries.

title: Joestar
summary: Joestar is a Windows-based VulnHub machine. Attackers perform web enumeration to find credentials, exploit remote code execution, and escalate privileges using misconfigured services.

title: Alpha
summary: Alpha is an easy Linux box. Initial access is gained via exposed FTP credentials, followed by enumeration and privilege escalation using Sudo misconfigurations.

title: Basilisk
summary: Basilisk is a challenging Linux VM. SQL injection on the web application allows RCE, followed by enumeration and exploitation of a cronjob to gain root.

title: Gamma
summary: Gamma is a medium Windows machine. SMB enumeration reveals weak credentials allowing NTLM relay attacks, which are then used to escalate privileges.

title: Hydra
summary: Hydra is a Linux VM vulnerable to SSH brute force. Once access is obtained, enumeration and Sudo exploitation allow full system compromise.

title: Delta
summary: Delta leverages Docker misconfigurations. Local File Inclusion leads to RCE, and root is gained through misconfigured container mounts.

title: Echo
summary: Echo is a Windows machine with weak RDP credentials. Initial access via RDP is followed by privilege escalation through registry misconfigurations.

title: Beta
summary: Beta is an easy Linux box. Directory traversal exposes configuration files containing credentials, which are used for initial access.

title: Icarus
summary: Icarus requires exploiting JWT misconfigurations to gain admin access, followed by SSTI for reverse shell and root escalation through misconfigured scripts.

title: Zeta
summary: Zeta is a medium Windows box. Web enumeration and password spraying provide access, followed by RCE exploitation and privilege escalation via IIS misconfigurations.

title: Mimic
summary: Mimic is a Linux VM with web services and Sudo misconfigurations. Enumeration reveals weak scripts allowing user and root compromise.

title: Theta
summary: Theta is a hard Linux box. SMB null sessions are exploited to obtain hashes, which are reused for Pass-the-Hash attacks and privilege escalation.

title: Pegasus
summary: Pegasus is a challenging Windows VM. SMB enumeration and NTLM relay attacks provide initial access and privilege escalation.

title: Omega
summary: Omega is a Linux machine with a vulnerable web application. SQL injection allows reverse shell, followed by enumeration for root access.

title: Nemesis
summary: Nemesis is a Linux VM where enumeration leads to SSH access. Sudo misconfigurations are exploited to gain root privileges.

title: Lambda
summary: Lambda is a Windows box with weak RDP credentials. Attackers exploit registry misconfigurations to escalate privileges.

title: Odin
summary: Odin leverages Docker container misconfigurations. Attackers gain RCE inside the container and escalate privileges to root.

title: Phi
summary: Phi is an easy Linux box where directory traversal exposes credentials for SSH login, providing initial access.

title: Quasar
summary: Quasar is a Windows machine. Attackers use web enumeration and password spraying to obtain access, then exploit IIS misconfigurations to escalate privileges.

title: Rho
summary: Rho exposes a Git repository. Enumeration reveals secrets for SSTI exploitation, reverse shell, and root escalation.

title: Sphinx
summary: Sphinx is a hard Linux VM where SMB null sessions are exploited to steal hashes, reused for Pass-the-Hash attacks to gain root.

title: Tau
summary: Tau is a Windows box with weak RDP credentials. Attackers gain access and escalate privileges using misconfigured services.

title: Ursa
summary: Ursa is a Linux VM with SQLi vulnerabilities. Exploitation provides reverse shell access and privilege escalation to root.

title: Psi
summary: Psi leverages Docker misconfigurations. Attackers gain RCE, enumerate Sudo privileges, and escalate to root.

title: Vulcan
summary: Vulcan is a Windows VM. Attackers perform web enumeration, password spraying, and exploit RCE to escalate privileges.
